message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-upg.2.4","title":"Persist ActionPlans + step logs; expose via CLI/robot for explainability","description":"# Task: Persist ActionPlans and step logs

## Goal
Make ActionPlans and per-step execution logs durable and queryable for explainability, debugging, and replay.

## Requirements
- Storage:
  - persist ActionPlan (canonical JSON + hash) linked to workflow execution
  - persist step logs with:
    - step id/kind
    - policy decision summary
    - verification evidence references
    - errors with stable codes
- Query surfaces:
  - robot/MCP: fetch plan and step logs by workflow id
  - human CLI: view plan/logs with progressive disclosure

## Testing
- Storage integration tests:
  - insert plan + step logs and query roundtrip
  - schema migration stability

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a workflow execution id, wa can show:
  - the ActionPlan that was executed
  - the step-by-step results and evidence

Tests pass (storage roundtrip, async API, integration).  {"id":"wa-upg.2.5","title":"Tests: plan determinism, hashing, idempotency, and failure reporting","description":"# Task: Tests for ActionPlan/StepPlan

## Goal
Ensure ActionPlan/StepPlan are deterministic and safe to use as the core substrate for approvals and execution.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - canonical serialization is stable
  - plan hash stability across runs/platforms
  - plan validation catches invalid/incomplete plans
  - idempotency keys prevent double-apply on retry
- Integration tests:
  - workflow runner emits plan + logs; restart/resume preserves invariants

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover determinism, idempotency, and error reporting in a way that prevents regressions.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:16.924964213Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:29:28.218186511Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:29:28.218130716Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive test suite for ActionPlan/StepPlan (28 tests total):
- Hash stability tests: known value verification, excludes timestamps/metadata, includes workspace/title
- Idempotency key tests: differs by workspace, step_number, and action
- Validation tests: duplicate step IDs, unknown step references, invalid step numbers
- Canonical serialization tests: stability for Precondition, Verification, OnFailure, StepAction, WaitCondition
- Integration tests: complex plan with all features, JSON roundtrip, helper methods
All tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-tests","area-workflows","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.2.6","title":"E2E: plan preview + workflow execution logs (no sleeps, rich artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E plan preview + workflow execution logs

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that ActionPlan preview and execution logging work in a realistic run.

## Scenario
- Trigger a known workflow.
- Capture the ActionPlan preview (dry-run / prepare).
- Execute and verify:
  - plan is persisted
  - step logs exist and are queryable
  - failures (if induced) reference specific step boundaries

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - plan JSON
  - step logs JSON
  - concise human summary

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E produces deterministic plan and step logs.
- Artifacts are sufficient to debug without re-running interactively.

## Goal
Make the plan-first execution model understandable to future contributors and power users.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - what ActionPlans and StepPlans are
  - how verification and idempotency work
  - how plan hashing relates to approvals
  - how to add a new Step kind safely

## Acceptance Criteria
- A contributor can implement a new workflow step without inventing ad-hoc behavior.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3","title":"[EPIC] Deterministic time + quiescence (no sleeps; wait-for utilities)","description":"# [EPIC] Deterministic time + quiescence

## Mission
Eliminate flakiness by making timing deterministic:
- no fixed sleeps in E2E/integration tests
- bounded timeouts and explicit wait-for conditions
- quiescence detection to know when the system is "done"

## Why This Matters
A system that can't be tested reliably can't be trusted.
Terminal automation is already nondeterministic; we must control what we can:
- time
- ordering
- retries

## Core Concepts
### 1) Test clock
Introduce a `Clock` abstraction so we can:
- advance time deterministically in tests
- avoid wall-clock coupling

### 2) Wait-for utilities
Centralize retry/backoff patterns:
- `wait_for(predicate, timeout)`
- `wait_for_regex(pane, ...)`
- `wait_for_quiescence(signals, timeout)`

### 3) Quiescence
Define quiescence in terms of **observable signals** (not sleeps):
- watcher ingest queue depth == 0
- storage writer queue depth == 0
- no new segments/events for a bounded "quiet window"

## Scope
- Implement core utilities (Clock + wait-for) in shared code.
- Refactor existing tests/E2E scripts to use the utilities.
- Improve logs so timing failures are diagnosable.

## Success Criteria
- E2E scripts run reliably (no flakey sleeps).
- Failures in wait-for show:
  - what was being waited on
  - last observed state
  - elapsed time and retry schedule

## Testing
- Unit tests for Clock and wait-for utilities.
- Integration test that simulates slow producer/consumer and validates quiescence logic.
- E2E: rerun a representative scenario multiple times (or in a loop mode) without flakes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A shared wait-for library exists and is used by E2E scripts.
- Quiescence is implemented with explicit signals and bounded timeouts.
- Logs for timing failures are sufficient to diagnose the cause.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.1","title":"Design: Clock abstraction + quiescence signals and contracts","description":"# Task: Design deterministic timing + quiescence

## Goal
Specify the contracts for:
- `Clock` abstraction
- wait-for utilities
- quiescence signals and semantics

## Requirements
- `Clock` contract:
  - `now()` (monotonic preferred)
  - `sleep(duration)` (or equivalent)
  - test clock can be advanced without sleeping
- Wait-for contract:
  - bounded deadline
  - exponential backoff with caps
  - structured error on timeout (include last observed values)
- Quiescence contract:
  - required signals (queue depths, last-write timestamps)
  - quiet window definition
  - interaction with "slow consumer" and backpressure

## Acceptance Criteria
- Design is specific enough to implement without ambiguity.
- Contracts make it hard to accidentally reintroduce fixed sleeps.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.2","title":"Implement wait-for utilities (no fixed sleeps) + structured timeout errors","description":"# Task: Implement wait-for utilities

## Goal
Provide a shared wait-for library used across unit/integration/E2E tests and optionally in production control loops.

## Requirements
- APIs:
  - `wait_for(predicate, timeout, backoff)`
  - `wait_for_value(query, expected, timeout)`
  - `wait_for_quiescence(signals, timeout)` (may call into wa-upg.3.3)
- Timeout errors include:
  - what condition was expected
  - last observed state
  - retries attempted and elapsed time
- Backoff strategy is configurable but defaults to sane values.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - backoff schedule correctness
  - timeout error includes debug info

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scripts can import and use wait-for helpers.
- No new test code introduces fixed sleeps when a wait-for is appropriate.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:38:20.407553976Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:31.401863288Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-reliability","area-tests","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
